Appearances: Edward Suman, via ZOOM, for Michael Lewis and Marlon Ravago, Petitioners; Marco Quazzo, via ZOOM, for John Artates, via ZOOM, Objector.

The court queries counsel regarding the status of the settlement conference set with Judge Mary Wiss. Mr. Suman reports of meeting with Judge Wiss without reaching a settlement but she advised the parties that they could contact her directly if the parties wished to engage in additional settlement discussion. Mr. Quazzo believes that the parties made some progress with Judge Wiss and feels that most, if not all, of the claims can be settled with additional settlement discussions with Judge Wiss. Mr. Suman disagrees and believes that an evidentiary hearing might be required and estimates requiring two days. Mr. Quazzo offers testimony and believes that resolving the issue confirming trustees will lead to additional resolution. The court advises counsel that it intends to set Case Management Order and briefly lists some of the directives that will be included in the order. The court encourages the parties to continue settlement discussions.

After additional discussion, A Case Management hearing is set for Jun-15-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition Concerning Internal Affairs of the Trust is continued to the same date. Mr. Suman is instructed to monitor the Court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the Case Management Order and serve it on all parties who are entitled to notice.

Off Calendar Jul-27-2021 Continued To Sep-28-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elizabeth Ferguson, in court, and Marco Quazzo, via ZOOM, for John Artates, in court, Objector; Edward Suman, via ZOOM, for Michael Lewis and Marlon Ravago, Petitioners.

The court begins by asking counsel what the current value of the trust assets is. Ms. Ferguson reports that the current assets are held in a bank account and a piece of property. The court asks if the parties are amenable to participating in alternative dispute resolution (ADR). Mr. Suman requests his clients be appointed as trustees and that this matter be ordered to mediation or arbitration. Mr. Quazzo replies that he has not discussed mediation with his clients and that there remains the issue of an unrepresented party, Consuelo Artates. The court advises counsel that Consuelo should be involved in any settlement discussions. Mr. Quazzo replies that he is unsure if she would be amenable to participating in settlement discussions. The court advises the parties to discuss and consider participating in ADR and advises the parties regarding the ADR options offered by the court. The court instructs the parties to meet and confer regarding ADR and to report their positions regarding the preceding to the appointed Examiner via email by no later than August 10, 2021. The court then advises the parties that if no update regarding ADR is received by the court by the previously mentioned deadline, the parties should be prepared to advise the court how they wish to proceed at the next calling of the case.

The Petition Concerning Internal Affairs of the Trust is continued to September 28, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Petition Concerning Internal Affairs Of The Trust
Off Calendar Apr-20-2021 Continued To Jun-22-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Edward Suman, via ZOOM, counsel for Michael Lewis and Marlon Ravago, Petitioners; Marco Quazzo, via ZOOM, and Elizabeth Ferguson, appearing in-person, Co-counsel for John Artates, appearing in-person, Respondent.

At the outset, Mr. Suman is queried by the court as to why he believes that this is the proper venue for his petition. Mr. Suman responds that the property is located in San Francisco which makes this the proper venue. The court advises Mr. Suman that there are currently no allegations as to why the venue is proper and the fact that the property is located in San Francisco is not sufficient. The court advises Mr. Suman to research probate code 17000 et seq regarding venue and that he may need to file an amended petition, accordingly. The court also alludes to the fact that any disputes regarding the estate should be settled via arbitration, pursuant to terms stated within the trust. Ms. Ferguson believes that one of the co-successor Trustees is unfit to serve due to the fact that they have declared bankruptcy twice. Mr. Suman argues that qualifications for both of his clients have been submitted to the court and believes that they should both be appointed. After further discussion, the court advises counsel that if this matter does not proceed in a more timely matter, the court may consider appointing a professional fiduciary.

The Petition Concerning Internal Affairs is continued to June 22, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Any papers related to this hearing, must be filed by no later than June 11, 2021.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn; Clerk: Robert Woods; Bailiff: Deputy Tauscher. Not Reported.
